---
title: "NV Control Framework"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

## A modular Python framework for experimental control of diamond NV center systems

The **NV Control Framework** is a modern Python software platform developed to automate and control nitrogen-vacancy (NV) center experiments. It provides a scalable architecture that separates hardware, experiment logic, and user interfaces, enabling rapid development of new quantum sensing experiments while maintaining clean and reusable code.

::: {.tech-badges}

`Python`
`PySide6`
`NumPy`
`PyQtGraph`
`VISA`
`Quantum Machines`
`Pulse Streamer`
`Andor`
`IDS`
`JSON`

:::

<div class="hero-buttons">

[GitHub Repository](#){.btn .btn-primary}
[Software Architecture](#software-architecture){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Development Roadmap](#development-roadmap){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

</div>

---

# Project at a Glance

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Status

🟢 Active Development

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Language

Python

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Design

Modular Object-Oriented

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Platform

Cross Platform

:::

:::

---

# Why This Framework?

Modern quantum sensing experiments require synchronized control of scientific cameras, microwave sources, lasers, pulse generators, motion stages, and laboratory instrumentation. As experimental systems become more sophisticated, software often becomes difficult to maintain and extend.

The **NV Control Framework** addresses this challenge through a modular software architecture that separates experiment logic from hardware implementation. This enables new hardware and measurement protocols to be integrated with minimal changes to the overall codebase.

---

# Key Features

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### 🔬 Modular Hardware Control

Support for cameras, microwave sources, pulse generators, motion stages, and laboratory instrumentation through unified hardware interfaces.

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### ⚛ Experiment Framework

Reusable experiment modules designed for ODMR, Rabi, Ramsey, T₁/T₂, widefield imaging, and future quantum sensing techniques.

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### ⚙ Configuration Driven

JSON-based hardware configuration allows the same software to operate across different laboratory setups with minimal modification.

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### 💻 Modern Software Engineering

Object-oriented architecture, modular design, version control, automated testing, and long-term maintainability.

:::

:::

:::

---

# Software Architecture

The NV Control Framework follows a layered software architecture that separates user interaction, experiment management, hardware abstraction, configuration, and the physical experimental platform.

![](assets/architecture_v1.png){width=100% fig-alt="NV Control Framework Architecture"}

*Figure 1. High-level architecture of the NV Control Framework.*

---

# Current Capabilities

| Capability | Status |
|------------|:------:|
| Hardware Abstraction | ✅ |
| ODMR Measurements | ✅ |
| Widefield Imaging | ✅ |
| GUI Framework | 🚧 |
| Data Management | 🚧 |
| Quantum Machines OPX | Planned |

---

# Supported Hardware

Current hardware support includes:

- Andor Neo Scientific Camera
- IDS Event Camera
- Stanford Research Systems SG384 Microwave Source
- Swabian Pulse Streamer
- SmarAct XYZ Positioning Stages
- Keysight Power Supplies

**Planned**

- Quantum Machines OPX
- Additional scientific cameras
- Modular laser controllers

---

# Development Roadmap {#development-roadmap}

| Version | Milestone |
|----------|-----------|
| v0.1 | ODMR Framework |
| v0.2 | Graphical User Interface |
| v0.3 | Rabi Measurements |
| v0.4 | Ramsey Measurements |
| v0.5 | T₁ / T₂ Measurements |
| v0.6 | Live Imaging |
| v0.7 | Quantum Machines OPX Integration |
| v1.0 | Stable Public Release |

---

# Research Impact

The NV Control Framework provides a unified software platform for developing scalable quantum sensing experiments. By separating experiment logic from hardware implementation, it enables rapid prototyping, reproducible measurements, and long-term maintainability across evolving laboratory platforms.

The framework is being developed as a core software infrastructure for current and future research in quantum sensing, quantum magnetic imaging, and precision instrumentation.

---

# Open Source

The NV Control Framework is under active development.

The public GitHub repository will provide:

- Source code
- Installation instructions
- Documentation
- Tutorials
- Example experiments
- Release notes

**GitHub Repository:** *(Coming Soon)*

---

## Continue Exploring

- [← Research Portfolio](../index.qmd)
- [Research](../../pages/research.qmd)
- [Software](../../pages/software.qmd)
- [Home](../../index.qmd)
